title: Lava Bold N2 5G is here with a 6.75-inch screen and a 6,000mAh battery
description: After unveiling the Lava Bold N2 4G in India back in February, the Indian brand has now announced the 5G variant of the handset. The Bold N2 5G comes with a bigger battery and the Unisoc T8200 chipset. It sports a 6.75-inch LCD display that offers an HD+ resolution and a refresh rate of 120Hz. The screen features a waterdrop notch, which houses a 5MP front facing camera. The Lava Bold N2 5G is powered by the 6nm octa-core Unisoc T8200 SoC, which is paired with the Mali-G57 MC2 GPU. It is available with 4GB LPDDR4x RAM and 64GB UFS 2.2 storage. Lava is offering a 6,000mAh battery...

title: Google launches fake call detection in its Phone app
description: Google today announced fake call detection for the Phone by Google app. This protects you from what the company refers to as "the growing threat of impersonation scams", which are now using AI deepfakes. Here's how these scams work. First, the scammers spoof the originating phone number of, say, one of your family members, "routing calls through internet-based software to make it appear as though the call is originating from a familiar contact", Google says. Then they use "easily accessible" AI deepfake technology to sound exactly like "an authority figure, family member, or...

title: Realme P4R's key specs, design, price range, and launch date revealed
description: Realme has announced it will unveil the Realme P4R in India on June 10 at 12 PM local time. The Realme P4R will be powered by the Dimensity 6300 SoC, run Realme UI 7.0 out of the box, and offer up to 256GB of storage. The smartphone will feature a 144Hz display with 1,200 nits peak brightness, and fueling the entire package will be an 8,000 mAh battery, advertised to deliver three days of endurance on a single charge. The Realme P4R will support 45W wired charging, 27W reverse wired charging, and all-scenario bypass charging. Realme is advertising a "One Tap Cooling" feature that would...

title: Honor X7e silently launched with a 7,500mAh battery
description: Honor has silently introduced a new budget 4G smartphone in select global markets. The phone’s called the Honor X7e and its key selling point is the large 7,500mAh battery. The Honor X7e is powered by the MediaTek Helio G81 Ultra chipset and runs Android 16-based MagicOS 10.0. As mentioned in the beginning, the highlight of the phone is its large 7,500mAh battery, which supports 45W wired charging. In some regions, the handset will come with a 7,000mAh battery. The device sports a 6.6-inch LCD display up front with an HD+ resolution and a refresh rate of up to...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra said to pack a bigger battery at the same weight as Fold7
description: After revealing new details about Samsung's upcoming wide-screen Galaxy Z Fold8, tipster Ice Universe has now shared fresh information about the successor to the Galaxy Z Fold7, which is widely rumored to launch as the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra. The tipster claims that the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra will maintain the same 215-gram weight as the Galaxy Z Fold7. However, the upcoming foldable is tipped to pack a larger 5,000mAh battery. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra leaked render Notably, Samsung offered a 4,400mAh battery on the Z Fold7. According to the tipster, the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra will...

title: Redmi K100 Pro specs leak, iPhone inspiration included
description: Back in March we got the first leaked details about the upcoming Redmi K100 Pro Max, and today the K100 Pro has received the same treatment. This device will allegedly come with an "ultra-high refresh rate screen" with an ultra-high resolution, which we assume means QHD+. The phone will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 SoC, unlike the Pro Max which will get the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6. The K100 Pro will have a 200MP main camera, a 50MP periscope telephoto camera, symmetrical dual stereo speakers with the strongest output in the segment, a large vibration motor, an ultrasonic...

title: Google is investing $80 billion in AI
description: Google's parent company Alphabet has just announced that it plans to raise $80 billion and invest that money in scaling its AI infrastructure. Alphabet will basically sell its own stock to get the cash, with a $30 billion public offering, a $40 billion program allowing the company to sell stock over time, and a $10 billion private placement investment from Warren Buffet's Berkshire Hathaway. Alphabet revenue grew 22% year-on-year to $110 billion in the first quarter of this year, while Google reached 350 million paid subscriptions in that quarter across its services. Alphabet...

title: Oppo is rumored to be working on an A-series smartphone with a 10,000 mAh battery
description: According to a new rumor from China's Digital Chat Station, Oppo is currently working on a mid-range A-series device with a whopping 10,000 mAh battery. The yet-unnamed phone will also allegedly have a large flat LTPS OLED screen with "1.5K" resolution. Unfortunately, the leaker hasn't mentioned the exact chipset it will be using, just that the chip is built on a 4nm process. Given the mid-range nature of the A-series, it's clearly not going to be a high end SoC. People on Weibo are speculating it might be the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4, or a Snapdragon 6 series SoC, or perhaps the MediaTek...

title: Asus unveils the VivoWatch 6 Plus with ECG and blood pressure monitoring
description: Today at Computex Asus unveiled a new smartwatch. It's called VivoWatch 6 Plus, and unfortunately the company didn't publish a whole lot of information about it so far. The device has a 1.43-inch AMOLED screen, sapphire crystal glass on top, and its case is made from titanium. The watch supports blood pressure measurement and ECG, as well as sleep breathing movement and gait analysis, "offering deeper insights into the risk of chronic disease and long-term health trends", the sparse official press release says. These features "combined with real-time health feedback transform the...

title: Oppo's upcoming ColorOS said to be borrowing some Liquid Glass UI elements from Apple
description: The next big release for Oppo's Android skin is ColorOS 17, which will presumably be based on Android 17. This is coming within the next few months, and today a rumor from China claims it will draw more than a little bit of inspiration from Apple's Liquid Glass design, introduced with iOS 26 last year. Unfortunately there aren't many details, but the source claims Oppo's version is more "liquid acrylic" than "liquid glass", since it has "relatively weak refraction" on the UI elements. The advantage of choosing this option is allegedly lower power consumption and better...

title: iPhone Ultra to sport a liquid metal hinge
description: Apple's first foldable smartphone, widely rumored to be coming this fall under the name iPhone Ultra, is now said to sport a liquid metal hinge. This information comes from one of the most prolific leakers over on Weibo. According to this source, the development of the iPhone Ultra is progressing rapidly, and prototypes have already been shipped to carriers worldwide for testing ahead of the launch. Despite the name, "liquid metal" is an alloy that isn't actually liquid, but unlike traditional metals, which have a rigid, crystalline atomic structure, it has a disordered,...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 and Fold8 Ultra's dummy units appear in side-by-side comparison photos
description: CAD-based renders of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Wide, now reportedly renamed Galaxy Z Fold8, revealed its design back in March. However, we recently saw a dummy unit of the Fold8 in a hands-on video, which gave us a better look at the wide foldable. The smartphone was also spotted in the wild, and now we are looking at more images, showing the dummy units of the Galaxy Z Fold8 and the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra (previously called Fold8) side-by-side. These images don't reveal anything new and only show how both foldables stack up in terms of design. The taller phone, previously called Fold8 and...

title: iPhone 18 Pro will offer two different battery capacities, claims new rumor
description: The upcoming iPhone 18 Pro is rumored to continue the battery strategy Apple introduced with the iPhone 17 Pro, offering different battery capacities depending on the region. According to tipster Digital Chat Station, the Chinese variant of the iPhone 18 Pro will feature a 4,056mAh battery, while the US model is said to pack a slightly larger 4,288mAh cell. iPhone 17 Pro Max and iPhone 17 Pro A similar approach was adopted with the iPhone 17 Pro lineup. The difference stems from the fact that Chinese models include a physical SIM card slot, whereas iPhone 17 Pro units sold in the...

title: Samsung is moving its US headquarters to Texas
description: Samsung’s US operations have been headquartered in New Jersey for over 40 years – and the majority of that time was spent in Ridgefield Park. Then in 2024, the company announced that it will move to Englewood Cliffs, also in New Jersey. But now Samsung is moving again and is leaving the state altogether. According to the Korea Times, Samsung will relocate its headquarters to Plano, Texas. Plano is just north of Dallas and is around 200 miles away from Taylor. Samsung has built several massive semiconductor fabs in Taylor and wants to build more. In 2024, it received a $6.6 billion...

title: This year Amazon Prime Day will be in June, here are the dates
description: For the last few years, Amazon Prime Day has been a July event – this year the retail giant has decided to bring it back to June, which was the traditional time frame before the switch to July. So, mark your calendars – Prime Day this year will be from June 23 to 26. Yes, it will be a four-day event, which is an idea that Amazon first tried out last year. You might not need to wait that long, however. You can visit the Prime Day page and check out some early deals. Here’s a preview of what’s coming – gaming and tech products from Sony and Logitech G will be up to half off. Laptops...

title: Asus re-enters tablet market with the new 12.2-inch Pad
description: Last month, a rumor suggested that Asus was preparing to re-enter the tablet market. Now, the company has officially confirmed its return to the category with the announcement of the new Asus Pad. The Asus Pad features a 12.2-inch dual-layer OLED display that offers a refresh rate of 144Hz, a 3:2 aspect ratio, 600 nits of brightness, and 2,800 x 1,840 pixels resolution. Under the hood, the tablet is powered by MediaTek’s Dimensity 8300 chipset, paired with 8GB of LPDDR5X RAM and up to 256GB of UFS 3.1 storage. Asus has also included a microSD card slot for storage...

title: Motorola Edge (2026) for US and Canada unveiled with smaller 6.3" display
description: We’ve been waiting for this one, but what we got is not what we expected – the Motorola Edge (2026) is a smaller phone with a 6.3” OLED display. Motorola US tends to do its own thing when it comes to the Edge series and the previous entries – Edge (2024) and Edge (2025) – had 6.6” and 6.7” displays, respectively. If anything, the trend was for screens to go bigger. Motorola Edge (2026) The Edge 60 Neo was available in Europe last year, but it never reached the US – like we said, Moto US does its own thing. But now there is a new player in the small mid-range segment – the...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 will weigh less than the Galaxy S26 Ultra
description: Samsung’s upcoming wide Galaxy Fold, which is rumored to be called simply Galaxy Z Fold8 became the subject of a series of new leaks. Across several posts on X, tipster Ice Universe detailed several key specs of the Fold8, including its weight, which is allegedly 201 grams. To put that number into perspective, Huawei’s Pura X Max, which holds similar proportions to the Fold8, tips the scales at 229 grams. Samsung’s flagship Galaxy S26 Ultra weighs 214 grams, despite not boasting a folding screen. We saw a similar, though not so apparent, discrepancy between the Galaxy 25 Ultra (218g)...

title: Google Pixel Watch 5 pops up in certification listing
description: Following up on the leaked images of the Pixel Watch 5, we now have a certification for the yet-to-be-released wearable from India’s BIS certification agency. The listing shows four model numbers: G0F3Y, G1XJ6, G25QD, and GFW3R. These are likely the Pixel Watch 5's 41mm and 45mm sizes and its two connectivity trims - Wi-Fi only and cellular. Google Pixel Watch 5 listing on BIS certification agency The scuba-diving leak from earlier this week showcased the 45mm version of the Pixel Watch 5. We also got confirmation that it features a heart rate sensor, a UWB chip and a pulse sensor,...

title: Redmi Turbo 5's India launch date revealed
description: Redmi unveiled the Turbo 5 in China back in January, and the company recently began teasing its arrival in India. After a series of teasers, the brand has now officially confirmed the smartphone’s India launch date. According to a teaser on Amazon India, the Turbo 5 will launch in the country on June 16. The promotional material also highlights the smartphone as the brand’s fastest Redmi to date. If the Redmi Turbo 5 coming to India is anything like the Chinese variant, you can expect it to be powered by the Dimensity 8500 Ultra SoC. In China, the phone comes with a 6.59-inch...
